title: The Best Laid Plans

jokes.text: Oh, baby, you all that and a bag of chips!
jokes.setup: Will trying to impress Monique with romantic talk
jokes.punchline: Using street slang 'bag of chips' as ultimate compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Will street language meets romantic moment - good character voice

jokes.text: Hey, I'm lying, I'm dying, you know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Monique asks if she's really 'all that'
jokes.punchline: Will's rhyming street poetry confession
jokes.explanation: Rhythmic wordplay fits Will's character perfectly, economical delivery

jokes.text: whatever it is that you serving... you better give me a double!
jokes.setup: Will building up another compliment
jokes.punchline: Restaurant metaphor for attraction
jokes.explanation: Good metaphorical structure, clear setup/payoff dynamic

jokes.text: I was just playing. You know how I be joking. I be joking.
jokes.setup: Will's over-the-top romantic talk
jokes.punchline: Awkward backtracking and repetitive nervous speech
jokes.explanation: Realistic nervous behavior, repetition shows character vulnerability

jokes.text: Man, something smells good. Oh, wait, that's me.
jokes.setup: Commenting on a good smell in the room
jokes.punchline: Realizing it's his own cologne
jokes.explanation: Efficient misdirection, classic setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: I don't smell anything. Oh, that's funny. It's a lot lighter than it was when I put it on.
jokes.setup: Carlton can't smell Will's cologne
jokes.punchline: Will's oblivious explanation that it's evaporated
jokes.explanation: Nice logical absurdity, Will missing the obvious implication

jokes.text: six or seven hours later, we'll come back down again
jokes.setup: Will explaining his romantic plans euphemistically
jokes.punchline: Absurdly long timeframe boast
jokes.explanation: Typical teenage male braggadocio, fits Will's character but somewhat crude

jokes.text: You don't know what I'm saying.
jokes.setup: Will's sexual innuendo falling flat
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the family's uncomfortable silence
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan acknowledgment of awkward moment, very economical

jokes.text: Well, you know how I'm always borrowing your things? Well, you don't have to worry about this one.
jokes.setup: Hilary asked about how her dress looks
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about never wanting to borrow it
jokes.explanation: Perfect mean-girl delivery, great character moment for shallow Hilary

jokes.text: To the restaurant. Le... Chez... Mc...
jokes.setup: Philip trying to remember fancy restaurant name
jokes.punchline: Struggling with French restaurant names
jokes.explanation: Standard absent-minded husband bit, serviceable but not special

jokes.text: I'm guessing they're not for me. I haven't put out in years.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey delivering flowers to the house
jokes.punchline: Dry sexual innuendo about his own romantic life
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of butler propriety, perfectly dry Geoffrey delivery, unexpected and quotable

jokes.text: Not anymore.
jokes.setup: Will mentions flowers were for Hilary
jokes.punchline: Philip's matter-of-fact theft admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy, deadpan delivery of morally questionable act

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, you lied to your woman.
jokes.setup: Will witnessing Philip's flower deception
jokes.punchline: Calling out the hypocrisy in street language
jokes.explanation: Good character voice, calling out authority figure's hypocrisy

jokes.text: My man, give it up top.
jokes.setup: Will approving of Philip's deception
jokes.punchline: Requesting high-five for morally questionable behavior
jokes.explanation: Consistent with Will's street attitude, moral flexibility for comedic effect

jokes.text: Jean-Claude Van... Damn, I'm fine.
jokes.setup: Will looking at himself in mirror, starting to say Jean-Claude Van Damme
jokes.punchline: Switching to complimenting himself mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay misdirection, perfect Will vanity, very quotable and smooth delivery

jokes.text: Oh, if you need me, I'll be dodging winos at the bus station.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey announcing he's leaving
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning dangerous evening plans
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey dry delivery of absurd situation, good character consistency

jokes.text: I told him I was going to church.
jokes.setup: Monique saying her father would kill her for being out with a boy
jokes.punchline: The lie she told to get out of the house
jokes.explanation: Standard teen deception joke, ironic given what Will is planning

jokes.text: The Lord is good.
jokes.setup: Monique mentioning church as her alibi
jokes.punchline: Will's mock religious gratitude for the deception
jokes.explanation: Good ironic response, economical delivery, fits Will's opportunistic character

jokes.text: And I ain't even oiled up yet, you know?
jokes.setup: Monique calling Will slick
jokes.punchline: Implying he can get even more smooth/slick
jokes.explanation: Plays on double meaning of 'slick', good Will swagger

jokes.text: Maybe you'd like to go upstairs and I could do my stand-up.
jokes.setup: Monique saying humor turns her on
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about 'standing up' sexually
jokes.explanation: Solid double entendre, economical setup/payoff but somewhat crude

jokes.text: Now, why waste time going upstairs? See, this way whenever you pass the couch, you'll think of me.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting they go upstairs
jokes.punchline: Monique's forward counter-suggestion with romantic reasoning
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal, Monique being more forward than expected

jokes.text: Maybe I could get some memories of you in the kitchen and the Jacuzzi, too.
jokes.setup: Monique's romantic couch memory idea
jokes.punchline: Escalating to multiple rooms including Jacuzzi
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation joke, reveals wealth of Banks household

jokes.text: Watch yourself, girl.
jokes.setup: Monique's increasingly sexual suggestions
jokes.punchline: Will's street warning/appreciation
jokes.explanation: Pure Will voice, economical reaction to escalation

jokes.text: Mama knows, child.
jokes.setup: Sexual tension building between Will and Monique
jokes.punchline: Monique's confident maternal/worldly response
jokes.explanation: Good character voice, confident female energy

jokes.text: Look, Monique, I'd love to talk, but there ain't no blood in my brain.
jokes.setup: Monique asking if Will understands her traditional values
jokes.punchline: Will's crude but honest physiological explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures teenage male frustration, honest and crude in classic Will style

jokes.text: No nookie last night?
jokes.setup: Will describing his hyperactive morning routine
jokes.punchline: Carlton's immediate diagnosis of sexual frustration
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff, Carlton's unexpected worldliness, very economical

jokes.text: Let's see, a week from Friday, it will be 18 years.
jokes.setup: Will asking how long Carlton usually gives girls to 'come around'
jokes.punchline: Carlton's precise calculation of his dry spell
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - Carlton's precision about his romantic failures, very quotable

jokes.text: Oh, cars!
jokes.setup: Will asking to talk about 'cars' (euphemism for sex)
jokes.punchline: Philip's delayed realization and awkward enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Classic parental awkwardness about sex talk, good timing

jokes.text: Oh, God, this car isn't in trouble, is it?
jokes.setup: Extended car metaphor for sex talk
jokes.punchline: Philip's fatherly concern about 'car troubles' (pregnancy)
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor extension, Philip's parental anxiety showing through

jokes.text: Never been off the lot as far as I can tell.
jokes.setup: Philip asking if Will's 'car' is in trouble
jokes.punchline: Will's admission he's still a virgin using car metaphor
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor continuation, economical way to reveal character information

jokes.text: then you throw a rod, you flood your carburetor, you crack your block
jokes.setup: Philip giving car advice as sex metaphor
jokes.punchline: Increasingly violent car problems as sexual consequences
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation of car metaphor, gets a bit overextended

jokes.text: No sex before marriage.
jokes.setup: Extended car metaphor confusion
jokes.punchline: Philip abandoning subtlety for direct statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic frustration, Philip giving up on metaphor, very economical

jokes.text: I've been doing that since the '80s. It don't work no more.
jokes.setup: Philip suggesting cold showers
jokes.punchline: Will's testimony about ineffectiveness
jokes.explanation: Good callback to decade reference, honest teenage perspective

jokes.text: I know I'm talking to teenage hormones from hell
jokes.setup: Philip trying to give mature advice
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging the futility with colorful description
jokes.explanation: Good parental exasperation, quotable phrase about teenage sexuality

jokes.text: We both mens here, you know? We got ugly needs.
jokes.setup: Will trying to get Philip to understand
jokes.punchline: Crude but honest male bonding attempt
jokes.explanation: Raw honesty, good Will voice, 'ugly needs' is memorable phrasing

jokes.text: and for the republic in which it stands
jokes.setup: Jazz performing fake wedding ceremony
jokes.punchline: Mixing wedding vows with Pledge of Allegiance
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist mixing of formal texts, shows Jazz's incompetence

jokes.text: Get busy.
jokes.setup: Jazz pronouncing them married
jokes.punchline: Crude command to consummate
jokes.explanation: Very economical, fits Jazz's crude character, but predictable

jokes.text: Hey. This is God's crib. Take that in the street where it belongs.
jokes.setup: Will trying to pay Jazz for fake ceremony
jokes.punchline: Jazz's sudden piety about money in church
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy - Jazz being selective about moral lines

jokes.text: Any minute now that minister's going to realize his house ain't on fire.
jokes.setup: Jazz and Will needing to leave quickly
jokes.punchline: Revealing how they got the church/minister
jokes.explanation: Good revelation of their scheme method, shows criminal creativity

jokes.text: Granted, my brother, we're good, but people can still see us.
jokes.setup: Will declaring them invincible
jokes.punchline: Jazz's practical reality check about visibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of Will's grandiosity, Jazz showing street smarts

jokes.text: we only got 18 hours left before checkout.
jokes.setup: Monique wanting to slow down on wedding night
jokes.punchline: Will's practical concern about hotel time limits
jokes.explanation: Good practical comedy, Will's priorities showing through romantic moment

jokes.text: These damn buttons. You got some scissors, girl?
jokes.setup: Will trying to undress for wedding night
jokes.punchline: Frustrated request for tools to bypass buttons
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy, Will's impatience, relatable frustration

jokes.text: Girl, be gentle.
jokes.setup: Monique saying she'll make him happy
jokes.punchline: Will's mock vulnerability request
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual role reversal joke, predictable

jokes.text: Honey, I meant for the rest of our lives.
jokes.setup: Sexual context of making Will happy
jokes.punchline: Monique's romantic long-term perspective
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Will's immediate and Monique's long-term thinking

jokes.text: Yeah, come on, baby, let's get naked. This thing open from the back or the front?
jokes.setup: Monique talking about sanctity of marriage
jokes.punchline: Will's crude interruption with practical clothing question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will character moment - missing emotional point completely

jokes.text: What a coincidence 'cause there's something I want to give you, too.
jokes.setup: Monique saying she has something to give Will
jokes.punchline: Will's sexual double entendre response
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual innuendo, consistent with Will's one-track mind

jokes.text: Thanks, a little box.
jokes.setup: Monique giving Will meaningful heirloom
jokes.punchline: Will's dismissive, surface-level observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Will missing emotional significance, very economical

jokes.text: Oh, damn!
jokes.setup: Monique's heartfelt speech about trust and love
jokes.punchline: Will's moment of conscience hitting
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of realization, extremely economical, shows Will's moral struggle

jokes.text: Sorry, Grampy.
jokes.setup: Will making excuse to leave the hotel room
jokes.punchline: Apologizing to dead great-grandfather for the deception
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to heirloom, shows Will's guilt, very economical and quotable

jokes.text: You know, my child, I've never known the pleasures of the flesh... but I could be convinced to give up the priesthood for you.
jokes.setup: Jazz hitting on woman at bar in priest costume
jokes.punchline: Offering to abandon vows for sex
jokes.explanation: Good sacrilegious humor, fits Jazz's shameless character

jokes.text: Don't go away baby. Bartender, another Olde English for the lady.
jokes.setup: Jazz being interrupted while hitting on woman
jokes.punchline: Ordering cheap malt liquor for his 'date'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz class/taste humor, Olde English is specific and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Now, get a bottle of whiskey, liquor that girl up... and take advantage of her. Be a man for God's sake.
jokes.setup: Will having moral qualms about deceiving Monique
jokes.punchline: Jazz's terrible advice to get her drunk
jokes.explanation: Dark humor but crosses into problematic territory, consistent with Jazz's moral vacuum

jokes.text: Well, to be honest, my wife took it.
jokes.setup: Will explaining to Uncle Phil about the missing Mercedes
jokes.punchline: Casually referring to Monique as 'my wife'
jokes.explanation: Good casual revelation that sets up family's confusion

jokes.text: Will and Monique stole a Mercedes, and ran over somebody's wife.
jokes.setup: Family trying to piece together Will's phone call
jokes.punchline: Carlton's completely wrong but logical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic, perfect Carlton overthinking, creative misinterpretation

jokes.text: Obviously, Will has been seeing a woman named Mercedes, Monique found out... and I'm still working on the wife part.
jokes.setup: Hilary offering alternative theory
jokes.punchline: Admitting confusion mid-explanation
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary logic attempt, honest about her confusion

jokes.text: Well, I think Will married Monique just to get her into bed... and when she found out, she got so mad she took off in Dad's Mercedes.
jokes.setup: Ashley offering her theory
jokes.punchline: Getting exactly right what happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect youngest-child wisdom, cutting through adult confusion with truth

jokes.text: Get real!
jokes.setup: Ashley's accurate theory about Will's behavior
jokes.punchline: Carlton's disbelief at the truth
jokes.explanation: Good ironic rejection of truth, Carlton's naivety showing

jokes.text: I don't know, Uncle Phil. I'm out of control. Please stop me before I disobey again.
jokes.setup: Will appearing genuinely remorseful
jokes.punchline: His mock-dramatic plea that reveals he's not really sorry
jokes.explanation: Good Will sarcasm - the over-the-top fake remorse with a clever twist

jokes.text: I'm a victim. I'm a victim of my imagination.
jokes.setup: Will trying to explain his behavior to Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Blaming his own creativity for his schemes
jokes.explanation: Good Will logic for avoiding responsibility, creative excuse-making

jokes.text: You know, that's radical, Uncle Phil, but it just might work.
jokes.setup: Philip saying he won't punish Will
jokes.punchline: Will's enthusiastic misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will missing the threat, treating mercy as experimental parenting

jokes.text: Be afraid, Will. Be very afraid.
jokes.setup: Philip explaining his delayed punishment strategy
jokes.punchline: Menacing movie reference threat
jokes.explanation: Good movie reference (The Fly), menacing but comedic, quotable delivery

jokes.text: I got that effect on women, you know.
jokes.setup: Monique fainting/reacting to something off-screen
jokes.punchline: Will's egotistical interpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Will ego moment, good end-of-episode character beat

tmdb_overview: When Monique refuses to have sex until she's married, Will stages a mock wedding ceremony with D.J. Jazzy Jeff as the preacher.
